description: CNS MPO desirability score, exactly as published in Wager et al. 2010 (v1). Six equally weighted physicochemical properties, each transformed to a desirability value T0 in [0,1]; the total is the SUM of the six T0 values and ranges 0-6.

six_properties_and_scale.paraphrase: Establishes the six inputs (ClogP, ClogD7.4, MW, TPSA, HBD, most-basic pKa) and that the score is their equally weighted sum on a 0-6 scale.
six_properties_and_scale.quotation_withheld: Wager 2010 is ACS-copyrighted and not in the PMC open-access subset. The locator is given so the passage can be read at the source.

function_shapes_and_summation.paraphrase: Establishes the transform shape per property: monotonic decreasing for ClogP, ClogD, MW, HBD and pKa; a hump for TPSA. Each yields a desirability T0 in [0,1], all weighted equally.
function_shapes_and_summation.quotation_withheld: Wager 2010 is ACS-copyrighted and not in the PMC open-access subset. The locator is given so the passage can be read at the source.
function_shapes_and_summation.location: Results and Discussion, 'A CNS Multiparameter Optimization and Desirability Score'

linear_interpolation.paraphrase: Establishes the interpolation rule: a monotonic function is 1.0 up to x1, 0.0 beyond x2, and linear between the two inflection points; a hump has one desirable region between two undesirable ones. These are the x1/x2 encoded per property below.
linear_interpolation.quotation_withheld: Wager 2010 is ACS-copyrighted and not in the PMC open-access subset. The locator is given so the passage can be read at the source.

calculators_used_by_the_authors.paraphrase: Establishes which commercial packages produced each published input (BioByte for ClogP; ACD/Labs for ClogD7.4 and pKa; a cited method for TPSA) — i.e. the calculator identity is part of the method, not an implementation detail.
calculators_used_by_the_authors.quotation_withheld: Wager 2010 is ACS-copyrighted and not in the PMC open-access subset. The locator is given so the passage can be read at the source.
calculators_used_by_the_authors.consequence: Defines the published method for each property input; see calculator_policy_v1.json. RDKit implements neither ClogD(7.4) nor most-basic pKa and must never be substituted for them.

score_is_not_a_measurement.paraphrase: The authors state the algorithm may help IDENTIFY compounds with a higher probability of success — a design-space heuristic, not a measurement.
score_is_not_a_measurement.quotation_withheld: Wager 2010 is ACS-copyrighted and not in the PMC open-access subset. The locator is given so the passage can be read at the source.
score_is_not_a_measurement.consequence: CNS MPO is a design-space desirability score. It is NOT a measured brain concentration, NOT a probability of CNS exposure, and NOT an NEBPI class. Stage 4 never promotes it to any of those.

table1_provenance.table: Table 1. The CNS MPO Properties, Functions, Weighting, Value Range and Parameter Ranges.
table1_provenance.parameters_encoded_in: properties[] — transform, weight and the x1/x2 inflection points
table1_provenance.note: The table itself is NOT reproduced here: the numeric parameters are the method and are encoded per property; the published table is ACS-copyrighted expression.

total.note: total_raw is the exact sum. total_published is total_raw rounded to 1 decimal (ROUND_HALF_UP) to match the precision reported in Wager Table 2. Neither is a probability, a measured concentration or an NEBPI class.

provenance.table: Table 2. CNS MPO Scores and Individual Transformed Scores (T0) for Selected Drugs.

golden_examples.scope_limitation: Wager Table 2 publishes the six TRANSFORMED component values (T0) and the total, but NOT the six raw property values (ClogP/ClogD/MW/TPSA/HBD/pKa) that produced them. The Supporting Information that holds the raw values for the full drug set is behind the ACS paywall (https://pubs.acs.org/doi/suppl/10.1021/cn100008c returned HTTP 403 on 2026-07-11). Therefore these goldens can validate equal weighting, the 0-6 summation and the published rounding, but they CANNOT validate the raw-property -> T0 transforms end-to-end. The transforms are validated separately against the published Table 1 inflection points. An end-to-end compound golden requires raw published property values and remains DEFERRED to independent review: Stage 4 holds no primary-source bytes for one. See unverified_derived_regression_example, which is an internal regression fixture and is explicitly NOT such a golden.
golden_examples.not_a_spot_claim: These rows are Wager et al.'s published values for those compounds. They are used ONLY as arithmetic goldens for the scoring engine. Spot makes no brain-permeability, safety or suitability claim about any of these drugs.

prohibited_interpretations: CNS MPO >= 4 does not mean the drug is brain-penetrant.
CNS MPO is not a measured brain concentration and not Kp,uu,brain.
CNS MPO is not a probability.
CNS MPO alone can never produce an NEBPI Part-II class (see nebpi_grossman2026_v1.json; physicochemical characteristics are a Part-I input, not a Part-II branch).
No imputation: if any of the six inputs is absent, non-finite, or sourced from a disallowed calculator, the score is incomplete and the total is null.

unverified_derived_regression_example.what_this_is: An internal regression fixture: six raw property values pushed through THIS implementation's Wager-2010 Table-1 transforms, whose expected outputs were recorded from that same implementation. It pins the arithmetic against future edits. That is all it does.
unverified_derived_regression_example.what_this_is_NOT: It is NOT a published golden, NOT primary-source validation, and NOT a validated or universal 4.5 cutoff. 4.5 is the TOTAL of this one worked example on the 0-6 desirability scale; it is not a threshold of any kind. Nothing in Stage 4 may cite this row as evidence that the implementation reproduces a published result.

attributed_to.claimed_table: Wager et al. 2016, Table 2 (worked CNS-MPO desirability example)
attributed_to.why_unverified: No 2016 article or table bytes were ever acquired or hashed: pubs.acs.org returned HTTP 403 and the DOI is not in PMC. These six values were copied from a prior audit report, not transcribed from primary-source bytes. Because they are then pushed through the very transforms they are supposed to check, agreement proves only that this implementation is self-consistent — it is circular, and it cannot corroborate itself.
attributed_to.how_to_promote_it: Acquire the lawful public 2016 article/table bytes, record the retrieval locator and raw SHA-256 in method/sources.json, transcribe the row independently FROM those bytes, and only then may this become a published end-to-end golden.
